personaChecks.evidence: Front matter declares `persona: developer`. Unchanged from attempt 1. The page lives under `developers-and-admins/development/` and is entirely about ASP.NET Identity configuration with C# code samples. Developer-only targeting is correct.

Attempt 2 changed only line 53: `.AddRoleStore<ApplicationRoleStore>()` → `.AddRoleStore<ApplicationRoleStore<ApplicationRole>>()`. The addition of the `<ApplicationRole>` generic type parameter is entirely within a C# code block. This is a compile-correctness fix that makes the code sample MORE appropriate for the developer audience (developers expect compile-ready code). No prose was altered. Tone remains consistently technical: exact API names, NuGet package references, `Program.cs` configuration, OAuth/OIDC terminology.

Depth unchanged from attempt 1. The code sample on lines 46–65 shows a complete, compile-ready ASP.NET Identity configuration block. Not too shallow (shows full configuration chain) and not too deep (no internal implementation details). The generic type parameter fix improves depth accuracy — developers can copy-paste without compilation errors.
